one_line_takeaway: FPCC improves adversarial robustness purely through clean training by constraining latent 'feature patterns' (relative dimension orderings) toward learnable per-class prototypes, matching or beating static-defense SOTA with higher clean accuracy and zero inference overhead -- but only when its three components (SFM+CFS+PRO) are combined.
key_finding: FPCC delivers the highest clean accuracy among the compared static defenses while achieving robust accuracy competitive with or exceeding adversarial-training baselines, and it passes the gradient-obfuscation sanity checks. Ablations show the three components are only effective jointly: none of SFM, CFS, or PRO alone yields meaningful robust accuracy, but combined they jump robust accuracy from ~0 to 74.37% (VGG-16, PGD-L2 on CIFAR-10).
core_contribution: The paper's OWN contribution is FPCC (Feature Pattern Consistency Constraint), a clean-training adversarial defense for image classifiers that requires no adversarial examples during training and adds no inference-time overhead. It rests on the authors' observation that a correct prediction depends not on the absolute values of a latent feature vector but on its 'feature pattern' -- the relative magnitude ordering across dimensions, formalized as z-score normalization of layer features. FPCC bundles three OWN components: (1) Spatial-wise Feature Modification (SFM), which injects random uniform noise delta in (-eps,+eps) into inputs to simulate increased interferential features (explicitly NOT adversarially optimized noise, contrasted with PGD-style adversarial training); (2) Channel-wise Feature Selection (CFS), which randomly zeros a Bernoulli(gamma) subset of features at a shallower layer -- explicitly WITHOUT Dropout's 1/(1-gamma) rescaling, so the original (not scaled) features are constrained; and (3) Pattern-based Robustness Optimization (PRO), which adds an L1 loss pulling each sample's per-layer feature pattern toward a learnable per-class prototype pattern d^(l), jointly with cross-entropy (weighted by lambda). CFS+PRO pairs are inserted into multiple (preferably deeper) layers.
threat_model: Test-time evasion of CNN image classifiers via small imperceptible perturbations. Evaluated under white-box and black-box access, against L-infinity (eps=8/255) and L2 (eps=0.5) bounded attacks plus patch attacks. The defender is restricted to clean-example training only (no adversarial examples, no test-time generative purification). The paper also proactively addresses the gradient-obfuscation threat: it runs Athalye et al.-style sanity checks to argue its robustness is genuine rather than from masked gradients.
limitations: Stated by the authors: (1) the method is validated only empirically and lacks theoretical backing; (2) test-time adversarial-purification methods, though they do not raise a model's intrinsic robustness, still surpass FPCC in absolute robust accuracy. Reviewer-observed: on some cells FPCC does not win uniformly (e.g., CIFAR-10 WRN-28-10 AutoPGD-L2 62.84% trails SCORE's 63.40% and MARGIN's 62.10% is close), gains over the strongest adversarial-training baseline SCORE are modest on standard PGD/AutoPGD and are largest on patch attacks (PIM/PIM++); evaluation is confined to CIFAR-10/100 (no ImageNet-scale results in the main text); and CFS/PRO layer placement plus selection ratio are sensitive hyperparameters (Figs 3-4) whose full config is deferred to the supplement.

quantitative_results: CIFAR-10 WRN-28-10 FPCC: clean 88.64%, PGD L2/Linf 67.28/65.58%, AutoPGD L2/Linf 67.14/62.84%, EOT-PGD L2/Linf 66.90/65.72%, FGSM 69.33%, BIM 65.97%, MIM 66.49%, PIM 57.95%, PIM++ 59.01% (Table 1)
CIFAR-10 WRN-28-10: FPCC gives highest PGD-Linf robust acc 65.58% and highest PIM robust acc 57.95% vs baselines (next best PIM is MARGIN 38.46%)
CIFAR-10 ResNet-50: FPCC clean 87.65%, PGD-L2 66.92% -- 41.97 points higher than RHS (24.95%) and clean acc 3.21 points above PGDAT (84.44%)

CIFAR-100 VGG-16 FPCC: clean 59.87%, PGD L2/Linf 39.74/38.69%, AutoPGD L2/Linf 39.98/38.54%, EOT-PGD L2/Linf 39.69/38.04% (Table 2); clean acc +4.47 over SCORE (51.39%); AutoPGD-L2 +28.94 over PROC and +29.16 over RHS

Gradient-obfuscation (CIFAR-10, PGD-Linf, Table 3): FPCC black-box > white-box in all backbones (VGG-16 74.37 white / 80.64 black; ResNet-50 61.82 / 70.33; WRN-28-10 65.58 / 71.98); undefended Base ~0% under both
Input-gradient L2 norm non-zero, ruling out gradient vanishing (Table 4, CIFAR-10): FPCC 0.97/1.56/1.77 vs Base 0.38/0.55/0.46 for VGG-16/ResNet-50/WRN-28-10

Ablation (VGG-16, CIFAR-10, PGD-L2, Table 5): Base RA 0.00%; +SFM RA 0.11%; +CFS RA 17.62%; +PRO RA 2.82%; full FPCC (SFM+CFS+PRO) SA 89.42% / RA 74.37%; SFM replaced by adversarial examples SA 82.21% / RA 46.17%; CFS replaced by Dropout RA 3.07%; PRO replaced by center loss RA 21.79%
Perturbation budgets: Linf eps=8/255, L2 eps=0.5; EOT set to 10 for EOT-PGD
